message:
fix(types): invalidate PageData shared snapshot cache on mutation

The previous `PageData::as_bytes_mut()` implementation left the `OnceLock`
snapshot cache in place when handing out a mutable reference to the owned
bytes. Any subsequent clone of the `PageData` would then reuse that stale
snapshot rather than observing the freshly mutated owned buffer, which is
a silent correctness bug for any code path that mutates a page in place
and then later clones it to produce a downstream snapshot.

This change resets the `shared` OnceLock whenever `as_bytes_mut()` is
called on an owned page that has a cached snapshot. The owned byte vector
itself is preserved via `std::mem::replace` so that no allocation or copy
happens — only the cached `Arc` is dropped. Later clones will lazily
re-materialize the snapshot from the updated bytes.

The inline unit test `page_data_owned_reuses_owned_bytes` was inverted to
reflect the corrected contract (snapshot cache must be cleared after
mutation), and a new regression test
`page_data_clone_after_owner_mutation_observes_latest_bytes` captures the
full scenario end-to-end: take a snapshot, mutate the owner, take a
second snapshot, and verify that the two snapshots reflect the pre- and
post-mutation bytes respectively.
